timeline.title: Day 1 Launch
timeline.detail: Running Late Dynasty officially launches for CFB 27 with private league setup, cross-play, and the first advance posted for July 10 at noon EST.

timeline.title: Conference Boards Set
timeline.detail: ACC, Big Ten, BIG XII, SEC, and Independent team boards are posted for the CFB 27 setup.

timeline.title: CFB 27 Draft Night
timeline.detail: The new era starts with a public draft order and team selections, beginning with Ohio State at pick one.

timeline.title: Stay Ready System
timeline.detail: Offseason pacing formalizes the league motto: move fast, stay ready, and keep the dynasty rolling.

timeline.title: GOTW and League Culture Grow
timeline.detail: Game of the Week voting becomes part of the weekly culture around activity and big matchups.

timeline.title: Week 0 Era Begins
timeline.detail: Announcements and ready-to-advance tracking begin, setting the rhythm that becomes the league identity.

timeline.title: Highlights Channel Comes Alive
timeline.detail: Streams, clips, and highlight links start turning the dynasty into more than standings: it becomes a weekly show.

timeline.title: Server Opens
timeline.detail: The league Discord opens with a general welcome message, giving the dynasty a home base before the first public advance posts.

brand.name: Running Late Dynasty
brand.privacy: Public league site for Running Late Dynasty CFB 27.
brand.footer: Perfect Timing Gaming • Powered by D1SN • Credits: Professor Zoom

sitemap.title: Splash Gate
sitemap.summary: Full magazine-cover splash page leading into the Dynasty Hub.

sitemap.title: Dynasty Hub
sitemap.summary: Command center with league status, headlines, featured games, health, and timeline.

sitemap.title: Coach Cards
sitemap.summary: Current coach directory with team, conference, record, and scheme details.

sitemap.title: Teams
sitemap.summary: Merged team/user hub with active member cards, coach-card links, conference board, and open-team pool.

sitemap.title: Schedule
sitemap.summary: Live user and CPU schedule grouped by week.

sitemap.title: History
sitemap.summary: Timeline from the CFB 26 roots into the CFB 27 Day 1 era.

sitemap.title: Media Center
sitemap.summary: Merged streams, clips, highlights, official YouTube playlist, and coach collaborator invite.

sitemap.title: Archive
sitemap.summary: Database of past index splash covers and weekly issue images, starting with Issue 1.

culture_notes: The league began as a CFB 26 Discord dynasty with weekly advances and public announcements.
Streams, clips, highlight links, and GOTW voting turned the league into a weekly show.
The CFB 27 launch keeps the old pace and public archive structure while adding a cleaner hub for new users.

public_notes: Standings, results, rankings, schedules, and readiness update from the live league spreadsheet.

ranks.description: Displays the current identified teams from the live RUNNING LATE DYNASTY TOP-25 Poll in original numeric ranking order.
ranks.note: Unidentified CPU-controlled schools are omitted without renumbering the remaining teams.

gotw.description: Displays the current GOTW ID, ranked matchup, nomination result, runner-up, and prediction status.

status.editorNote: Live league data refreshes from the dynasty spreadsheet.

coverLines.title: The Season Is Underway
coverLines.detail: Live results, rankings, standings, and schedules update throughout the week.

coverLines.title: 32 Active Member Programs
coverLines.detail: ACC, Big Ten, BIG XII, SEC, and Independent teams are represented.

coverLines.title: 48-Hour Advances
coverLines.detail: Advance readiness and the next deadline come directly from the league dashboard.

coverLines.title: Every Week In One Place
coverLines.detail: Follow results, rankings, Game of the Week, streams, and league history.

statusCards.label: Advance Status
statusCards.detail: In-Game Weeks move on a 48-hour cycle. Second Advance is Sunday @ NOON EST

statusCards.label: Scheduling Status
statusCards.detail: Users should schedule within 24 hours after advance with a time or 2-hour window.

statusCards.label: Stream Status
statusCards.detail: User games should be streamed, screen shared, or recorded for admin review.

statusCards.label: GOTW Status
statusCards.detail: #1 Ohio State (1-0) at #7 Texas (0-0). Nomination closed with 13 votes; prediction voting has not started.

headlines.title: Florida State defeats Alabama in the Week 3 Game of the Week
headlines.summary: The Seminoles earned the Week 3 showcase win over Alabama.

headlines.title: 🎉 Congratulations to jrob9179 (Texas)
headlines.summary: Texas defeated Ohio State 59-13 to win the Week 2 Game of the Week.

headlines.title: Find every user team and coach
headlines.summary: Search the league by school, username, conference, record, or ranking.

watchlist.title: SEC gauntlet
watchlist.detail: 12 active member programs create the deepest conference board on the site.

watchlist.title: Rivalry week heat
watchlist.detail: Florida vs Florida State, Texas vs Texas A&M, Auburn vs Alabama, Michigan vs Ohio State, Washington vs Oregon, and more are already scheduled.

watchlist.title: Neutral-site flex
watchlist.detail: Texas vs Oklahoma at Cotton Bowl Stadium and Florida vs Georgia at Mercedes-Benz Stadium are marked as neutral-site games.

watchlist.title: League media
watchlist.detail: Follow streams, clips, highlights, and the official Running Late playlist.
